ch4_start.text: Klaxons. PA: 'Mess Hall lockdown. Mandatory rationing.' The station has stopped pretending this is routine. You can investigate several areas before the next lockdown cycle seals them.

ch4_hub.text: Sector A corridor. Bulkheads tick as pressure equalizes. Where do you go before the cycle ends?

choices.text: Mess Hall — Otto and the missing rations
Sector B access tunnel (still dry — for now)

ch4_mess_hall.text: Outside the sealed Mess Hall, Otto argues with a security drone. 'Someone is stealing the reserves!' He sees you and freezes — too long, too guilty.
ch4_mess_hall.speaker: Otto

choices.text: Confront him about the missing rations.
Ask calmly if anyone else has access to storage.
Search around him without accusing yet.

ch4_mess_search.text: While Otto is distracted, you recheck the pantry seam. If you missed evidence earlier, this is your second chance — crumbs, crayon wax, a blanket thread.

choices.text: Bag a crayon scrap from the shelf edge.

ch4_soft_ask.text: 'Just me and the inventory codes,' Otto mutters. 'Look — if I tell you, you have to promise. There's a kid. She's starving without me.'
ch4_soft_ask.speaker: Otto

choices.text: Promise to keep the secret — for now.
Demand proof before you believe a word.

ch4_confront_menu.text: Otto wrings a towel until his knuckles go white. 'I didn't poison anyone. I swear.' Accusing him will change everything.
ch4_confront_menu.speaker: Otto

choices.text: Accuse him of hoarding food out of greed.
Present the child's drawing and the inventory logs.
Accuse him of poisoning Miller with the stew.

ch4_accuse_greed.text: You call him a thief. Otto's face collapses into rage. David has him in the brig within the hour. No one checks the vents.
ch4_accuse_greed.speaker: Otto

ch4_accuse_poison.text: 'Miller was my friend!' Otto shouts as security hauls him away. The crew watches you with measuring eyes.
ch4_accuse_poison.speaker: Otto

ch4_accuse_correct.text: You lay out the inventory gaps, the drawing, the tiny bed. Otto sags. 'Her name is Maya. Her brother was on the drill team. I've been feeding her in the vents.'
ch4_accuse_correct.speaker: Otto

choices.text: Keep his secret. Ask to meet her.

ch4_report_maya.text: David logs an unauthorized civilian. Maya is dragged to holding. Otto looks at you as if you flooded the lower decks yourself.
ch4_report_maya.speaker: David

ch4_vent.text: Otto opens a hatch. A girl curls in the dark. 'I'm Maya,' she whispers. 'Please don't tell the Director.'
ch4_vent.speaker: Maya

choices.text: Promise to protect her, then return to hub.

ch4_hydroponics.text: Grow-beds are half-dead. On the filter mesh: a faint bioluminescent smear that is not algae. The same wrong light you saw in rumor of the cavern mud.

ch4_observation.text: Beyond reinforced glass: absolute black, and the memory of pressure. Your comms unit catches a fragment of the drill team's last open channel — already half-eaten by static.

choices.text: Kill the channel. Return to hub.

ch4_sector_b.text: Sector B still smells of coolant and dust — not seawater. Not yet. A maintenance stencil reads: STRUCTURAL WATCH — MILLER. The lower bulkhead ticks like a bad tooth.

choices.text: Note the structural watch tags and leave.
Pry a secondary panel for another bypassed relay fragment.

ch4_end.text: Rationing deepens. Trust thins. Whatever is in the walls has learned the sound of human desperation — and it is practicing. You will not get unlimited cycles to look around.
